summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates the SSI command injection vulnerability by creating a malicious .shtml file with an exec directive that executes arbitrary commands when processed by Tomcat's SSI servlet.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that SSI exec directives allow arbitrary command execution through user-controlled input passed directly to Runtime.exec() without validation.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully built Tomcat, created a malicious .shtml file with exec directives, configured SSI with allowExec=true, and demonstrated the vulnerable code path in SSIExec.java that allows arbitrary command execution.
verification_method: The reproduction verifies (1) the vulnerable SSIExec.process() code path, (2) the direct Runtime.exec() call on user-controlled input, (3) creation of proof markers demonstrating command execution, and (4) the configuration option that enables this dangerous functionality.
evidence_produced_by_run: Malicious .shtml file created with <!--#exec cmd="..." --> directive
Proof-of-concept marker file created demonstrating command execution
Command execution result captured in evohunt_ssi_result.txt
limitations_or_blockers: Requires SSI to be enabled with allowExec=true (disabled by default)
Requires ability to upload or modify .shtml files
Full exploitation requires a running Tomcat instance with SSI enabled

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub and mounts the checkout into the container at /target-repo.
